summary: There is no clinically supported relationship between serum angiotensin-converting enzyme levels and frequent severe hypoglycemia in Type 1 diabetes; instead, concerns arise solely from drug interactions involving ACE inhibitors with medications like sulfonylureas, which affect glucose control independently of baseline serum ACE concentration. The available evidence fails to show an association between actual serum ACE measurements and hypoglycemia outcomes.
